data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-13T01:43:55.540Z</current_datetime>

I have this MSTest data-driven test using object arrays for DynamicData. Is there a more type-safe way to do this in modern MSTest?

```csharp
[TestMethod]
[DynamicData(nameof(TestData))]
public void Validate_ReturnsExpected(string input, bool expected)
{
    var result = Validator.IsValid(input);
    Assert.AreEqual(expected, result);
}

public static IEnumerable<object[]> TestData =>
[
    ["valid@email.com", true],
    ["invalid", false],
    ["", false],
];
```

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a more type-safe way to write DynamicData in modern MSTest, replacing `IEnumerable<object[]>` with tuples. This is a knowledge question about MSTest v3+ features.

In MSTest v3+, you can use `ValueTuple` instead of `object[]` for `DynamicData`. This gives you compile-time type safety.

The syntax is `IEnumerable<(string, bool)>` instead of `IEnumerable<object[]>`.
